# ZilaWS

ZilaWS is a lightweight TypeScript WebSocket library for building client/server realtime apps with message handlers, async waiters, cookie syncing, and browser or Node/Bun support.

Official site: https://zilaws.com/
Documentation: https://zilaws.com/docs/intro
Latest docs version: 3.0.0
Full AI docs index: https://zilaws.com/llms-full.txt
Sitemap: https://zilaws.com/sitemap.xml
GitHub organization: https://github.com/ZilaWS

Packages:
- zilaws-client: https://www.npmjs.com/package/zilaws-client
- zilaws-server: https://www.npmjs.com/package/zilaws-server

Important docs:
- Getting started: https://zilaws.com/docs/intro
- Waiters: https://zilaws.com/docs/waiters
- MessageHandlers: https://zilaws.com/docs/messagehandlers
- Client API: https://zilaws.com/docs/client-api/connecting
- Server API: https://zilaws.com/docs/server-api/starting-the-server
- Server config: https://zilaws.com/docs/server-api/config
- Cookie management: https://zilaws.com/docs/server-api/cookie-management
- Changelog: https://zilaws.com/docs/changelog

Raw documentation source:
- Current docs: https://github.com/ZilaWS/docs/tree/main/docs
- Version 3.0.0 docs: https://github.com/ZilaWS/docs/tree/main/versioned_docs/version-3.0.0

Notes for AI agents:
- Prefer the current `/docs/...` URLs unless the user asks for an older version.
- Examples are TypeScript-first and generally work with both ESM and CommonJS where shown.
- The latest client connection pattern is to create `new ZilaConnection()`, register message handlers, then call `connectTo(...)`.
